page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.3xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon CPU operating at a frequency of 2.5 GHz. It features 12 dedicated physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, providing 12 vCPUs. The server is configured with 12.0 GB of memory, yielding a 1.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this server consistently in the average performance tier. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1426.0 and a multi-core score of 7074.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 15105.88. In memory bandwidth tests, it reaches 636.33 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 82.64 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. For specialized tasks, it processes static web serving at 13.6 GB/sec and handles Redis SET operations at 11.77 million operations per second. LLM inference benchmarks show average performance for small models but drop to weak performance for medium 7B models.

From a resource density perspective, the ecs.u1-c1m1.3xlarge offers a highly compute-centric profile with a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:1. This design is cost-efficient for workloads that require significant parallel processing power without demanding large memory footprints. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, and the 12 GB RAM limit represents a clear tradeoff for memory-intensive applications.

This server is suited for compute-bound tasks with low memory requirements, such as lightweight software compilation, text and image processing, static web serving, and parallelized batch processing. It is also capable of hosting small-scale database operations and handling lightweight microservices.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.3xlarge is an x86_64 compute-oriented server featuring 12 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 12.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1.0 GB per core memory ratio, and does not include local storage or GPU hardware. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 7074.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 15105.88. It demonstrates solid capabilities in static web serving at 13.6 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 11.77 million operations per second. Given its 1:1 CPU-to-RAM ratio, this server is highly cost-efficient for parallelized, compute-heavy workloads with minimal memory footprints, such as software compilation, image processing, and lightweight web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.3xlarge is an x86_64 server with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 12.0 GB of RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for compute-heavy workloads with low memory requirements.

tagline: A compute-focused, dedicated core server designed for parallel processing tasks requiring a minimal memory footprint and steady CPU performance.

12.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on a KVM hypervisor
Average benchmark performance with a Geekbench multi-core score of 7074.0
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
